name: Osuna, El Saucejo, and Martín de la Jara Loop
name_original: De Osuna a Martín de la Jara por El Saucejo y Los Corrales – circular
description: This moderate 36.8-mile road cycling route from Osuna to Martín de la Jara takes you through the picturesque Sevillian countryside.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Osuna traverse the picturesque Sevillian countryside, characterized by rolling hills, extensive olive groves, and cereal crop fields. The region offers diverse terrain, from mostly well-paved surfaces suitable for moderate rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation gains. This varied landscape provides options for different fitness levels, making it a compelling destination for road cyclists seeking scenic routes. Best road cycling routes around Osuna The most popular road cycling route is From Osuna to Martín de la Jara via El Saucejo and Los Corrales – circular, a 36.8 miles (59.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 48 minutes to complete. This route features moderate climbs through the rural landscape. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is From Osuna to Marchena - circular via La Puebla de Cazalla, a moderate 51.5 miles (82.8 km) path. This route offers a mix of open farmland and small town passages. Local road cyclists also love the Alcalá del Valle – Algámitas loop from La Pelusa, a 59.5 miles (95.8 km) trail leading through hilly terrain with significant elevation changes, often completed in about 5 hours 7 minutes. Road cycling around Osuna is defined by rolling countryside, vast olive groves, and smaller mountainous areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging routes with substantial climbs. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 180 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Osuna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Osuna?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes around Osuna, catering to various skill levels. These routes traverse the picturesque Sevillian countryside, offering diverse terrain from rolling hills to extensive olive groves and cereal crop fields.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Osuna?
A: Yes, Osuna offers a few easier routes, though the region is generally characterized by rolling hills and constant ups and downs. These routes often guide cyclists through the agricultural landscapes of La Campiña, providing a pleasant introduction to road cycling in the area.

Q: What kind of challenges can experienced road cyclists expect in Osuna?
A: Experienced cyclists will find plenty of challenging routes with significant elevation gains. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31486815" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá del Valle – Algámitas loop from La Pelusa&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 59.5-mile (95.8 km) trail with over 1800 meters of elevation change, leading through hilly terrain. Other routes, like El Puerto de la Encina track, feature continuous climbs to elevated points such as Pinalejo.

Q: Which road cycling routes offer the most scenic views around Osuna?
A: Many routes showcase the beautiful Andalusian rural landscape, characterized by vast cereal crop fields and traditional farmhouses. Routes that pass by the Corbones Reservoir, such as those leading towards El Puerto de la Encina track, offer attractive views of the water amidst rich flora and fauna. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4083606" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coto de las Canteras (Osuna)&lt;/a&gt; provides panoramic views of the area.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes in the Osuna area?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Osuna are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular circular options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995605523/from-osuna-to-martin-de-la-jara-via-el-saucejo-and-los-corrales-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Osuna to Martín de la Jara via El Saucejo and Los Corrales – circular&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995572193/from-osuna-to-marchena-circular-via-la-puebla-de-cazalla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Osuna to Marchena - circular via La Puebla de Cazalla&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical or natural attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Osuna's routes often integrate historical and natural features. You might encounter ancient drovers' roads leading to sites like the Roman Theatre and Roman Necropolis. The historic center of Osuna itself boasts monuments such as the Collegiate Church. For natural beauty, routes can lead to the Corbones Reservoir or near the endorheic lagoons, which are protected areas attracting various bird species. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4083606" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coto de las Canteras (Osuna)&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable historical site with great views.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Osuna?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling in Osuna, with milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot in Andalusia, so if cycling during this season, it's advisable to start early in the morning to avoid the midday heat. Winters are typically mild, making it possible to cycle year-round, though some days might be cooler.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling paths in Osuna?
A: While many routes feature moderate climbs and descents, some sections of the La Campiña track or El Alamillo track, particularly those passing by social orchards, can be suitable for families looking for gentler rides. It's recommended to check the specific route profiles for elevation and distance to ensure it matches your family's fitness level.

Q: Where can I find parking when starting a road cycling route in Osuna?
A: Many road cycling routes in Osuna often start and end at the Osuna Municipal Sports Center, which typically offers parking facilities. Additionally, parking may be available in the town center or near other common starting points, though it's always good to check local regulations.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Osuna?
A: Road cyclists frequently praise Osuna for its diverse terrain, which provides a good mix of moderate rides and challenging climbs. The picturesque countryside, with its extensive olive groves and cereal crop fields, along with the integration of historical sites and natural features like the Corbones Reservoir, makes for a highly engaging and scenic cycling experience. The region's authentic Andalusian rural charm is also a significant draw.

Q: Are there any less crowded road cycling alternatives around Osuna?
A: While Osuna's routes are generally not overly crowded, exploring some of the longer, more challenging routes that venture further into the countryside, such as the one from Osuna to Cañete la Real and Algámitas, might offer a more secluded experience. The GR-341 trail, part of the Caminos de Pasión network, also provides a blend of cultural discovery and scenic cycling on less-trafficked paths.
tips: The Osuna University School is an official center attached to the University of Seville that has been developing university activity (in this second stage) since 1993. In these almost twenty years, the Degrees in Labor Relations and Human Resources, Finance and Accounting, Double Degree in Labor Relations and Human Resources and Finance and Accounting, Degree in Nursing, Degree in Primary Education, Degree in Physiotherapy and Degree in Early Childhood Education. It is therefore an official center, fully adapted to the new framework of the European Higher Education Area. In addition, it has a headquarters of the Institute of Languages of the University of Seville, where you can study English, French and German.

2 km from the municipality is a beautiful lagoon - Laguna del Gosque declared a nature reserve. Thanks to the proximity of the Laguna de Fuente de Piedra, the presence of flamingos and mallards is common.

El Saucejo is a popular town for its fountains, for example the Fuente del Moro, classified as an Asset of Cultural Interest. In the center of El Saucejo you can see a beautiful church - the Iglesia de San Marcos Evangelista and a few meters away the Town Hall building, also painted in the typical colors of western Andalusia, white and yellow.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this cycling route in Osuna?
faqs.answer: Osuna, being the starting and ending point of this circular route, offers various parking options within the town. Look for public parking areas or street parking near the town center to begin your ride conveniently.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate due to its length of approximately 59.3 kilometers and an elevation gain of about 745 meters. While the surfaces are predominantly well-paved, it features moderate climbs. It is best suited for cyclists with good fitness levels rather than absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of well-paved roads, making it ideal for racebikes. You'll experience rolling hills, extensive olive groves, and vast fields of cereal crops as you traverse the picturesque Sevillian countryside.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and points of interest can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll cycle through the quintessential Sevillian countryside, characterized by agricultural landscapes. The route passes through charming settlements such as Martín de la Jara and El Saucejo. Starting in Osuna, you can also see the historic University of Osuna.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Sevillian countryside is best enjoyed during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the landscape is vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making cycling less comfortable.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: On average, cyclists complete this 59.3-kilometer route in about 2 hours and 48 minutes. This duration can vary depending on your fitness level, speed, and the number of stops you make.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is a road cycling route primarily on paved roads, it is generally not suitable for dogs to accompany you, especially for the entire distance. If you plan to bring a dog, consider alternative activities or shorter, off-road trails.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public road cycling route through the Sevillian countryside.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay along the route, particularly in the towns it passes through?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through several towns including Osuna, Martín de la Jara, and El Saucejo. These settlements offer opportunities to find cafes, restaurants, and potentially accommodation for refreshments or an overnight stay.

faqs.question: What gear should I bring for road cycling this route?
faqs.answer: Given it's a moderate road cycling route, you should bring your racebike, appropriate cycling attire, a helmet, water bottles, snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), and navigation tools. Sunscreen and sunglasses are also recommended, especially during sunnier months.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route briefly overlap with several official trails, including the Cañada Real de Ronda, Cañada Real de Ronda a Sevilla, Cañada Real de Osuna a Canete, Vereda de Calderona, and Vereda del Calvario.
